LanguageData.FCP_Meme_BoS_Codex_Supremacy.description: The Brotherhood of Steel operates under the strict tenets of the Codex, a sacred doctrine that demands the preservation and control of advanced technology. Under Elder Maxson, the Brotherhood has united the ideals of both the Outcasts' technological zealotry and Lyons' willingness to engage with outsiders - but on the Brotherhood's terms. Technology must be controlled, mutants and synths are abominations to be destroyed, and only through discipline and hierarchy can humanity be saved from itself. The Brotherhood is both shield and sword - protecting humanity while ensuring dangerous technology never falls into the wrong hands.
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_BoS_Codex_Supremacy.label: codex supremacy
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_BoS_Paladins.description: Paladins are the Brotherhood's elite warriors, clad in power armor and wielding the most advanced weapons. They are the tip of the spear in any Brotherhood operation, trained to engage the most dangerous threats head-on. A Paladin's duty is to lead from the front, inspire their squadmates, and demonstrate the Brotherhood's martial superiority.
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_BoS_Paladins.label: paladins of steel
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Lost_Hills_Codex_Eternal.descri: Lost Hills represents the original Brotherhood of Steel - the purest interpretation of the Codex as written by Roger Maxson himself. Technology must be preserved at any cost, outsiders are never to be trusted, and the Brotherhood's bloodlines must remain pure. Isolation is strength. The wasteland may burn, but the Brotherhood will endure, guarding humanity's technological legacy until the world is ready to rebuild - on Brotherhood terms.
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Lost_Hills_Codex_Eternal.label: the codex eternal
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Lyons_Shield.description: The Shield of the People represents everything the Lyons Pride stands for - warriors who protect the innocent and fight for those who cannot fight for themselves. They combine Brotherhood combat prowess with genuine compassion, serving as both soldiers and humanitarian workers.
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Lyons_Shield.label: shields of the people
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Lyons_Steel_Compassion.descript: Elder Lyons taught that technology is worthless if it doesn't serve the people. The Lyons Pride believes the Brotherhood must be more than collectors and hoarders - they must be protectors and builders. While still bound by the Codex's technical wisdom, they reject its isolationism. Technology should be preserved, but also deployed to help those in need. The wasteland doesn't need another group of tech-hoarders; it needs guardians who understand that saving humanity means actually helping humans, not just safeguarding artifacts.
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Lyons_Steel_Compassion.label: steel compassion
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Midwest_Steel_Vanguard.descript: The Midwestern Brotherhood emerged from the wreckage of their crashed airships with a revolutionary understanding: survival demands adaptation. Unlike the isolationists at Lost Hills, the Midwest chapter actively recruits tribals, wastelanders, and even former enemies into their ranks. Technology still matters, but so does building an army capable of holding territory and protecting the people within it. The Codex is a guide, not a chain. Expansion is not corruption - it is evolution.
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Midwest_Steel_Vanguard.label: the steel vanguard
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Outcast_Defenders.description: Defenders are Outcast specialists who combine superior technical knowledge with combat capability. They are responsible for securing technology from hostile forces and ensuring no advanced equipment falls into unworthy hands. Their dedication to the mission is absolute.
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Outcast_Defenders.label: defenders of the codex
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Outcast_True_Codex.description: The Outcasts are the Brotherhood's purists - those who refused to compromise the Codex when Elder Lyons went soft. Technology is everything. It's not about helping wastelanders or building communities; it's about preserving humanity's technological legacy before fools destroy it all. The Old World fell because people misused technology - the Brotherhood's sacred duty is to prevent that from happening again by controlling every piece of advanced tech in existence. Outsiders are liabilities at best, threats at worst. Only through absolute dedication to the mission can the Brotherhood fulfill its purpose.
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Outcast_True_Codex.label: true codex
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Legionism.description: Our people should dominate all others.
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Legionism.label: might makes right
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_EnclaveManifestDestiny.descript: The Enclave represents the continuation of the United States government, destined to reclaim and restore America. Only pure, untainted humans have the right to rebuild civilization. All others are aberrations to be removed or subjugated.
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_EnclaveManifestDestiny.label: Enclave manifest destiny
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Khan_Chemists.description: The Great Khans have perfected the art of chem production. Their chemists are revered members of the tribe, responsible for creating the drugs that fund the Khan's operations. A skilled chem maker is worth their weight in silver, and their knowledge is passed down through generations.
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Khan_Chemists.label: khan chemists
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Khan_Freedom.description: The Great Khans reject the chains of "civilized" society - no laws, no taxes, no rulers except the strongest among them. They value personal freedom above all else, but with that freedom comes responsibility to the tribe. Strength is respected, weakness is despised, and loyalty to your brothers and sisters is sacred. The Khans don't apologize for taking what they need - the wasteland belongs to those strong enough to claim it.
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Khan_Freedom.label: khan freedom
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_NCRDeserters.description: Once loyal to the New California Republic, these individuals have turned their backs on the old world order. Disillusioned by corruption and endless war, they now value survival, independence, and camaraderie above all else. They are wary of authority, skeptical of grand ideals, and quick to defend their own. Their code is one of mutual aid, hard-earned trust, and doing what it takes to survive in the wasteland.
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_NCRDeserters.label: NCR deserters
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_OldWorldBlues.description: The New California Republic (NCR) is a federal presidential republic founded in New California, comprised of five contiguous states and additional territorial holdings in Nevada and Baja California. The NCR emphasizes and strives to support a myriad of old world values, such as democracy, personal liberty, and the rule of law. It also aims to restore general order to the wasteland, the improvement and development of infrastructure and economic systems, and overarching peace between people. Similar to institutions of the old world it seeks to emulate, continued expansion has created challenges with territorial control, loyalty, and corruption that plague the Republic and serve to hinder its goals.
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_OldWorldBlues.label: old world blues
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Rangers.description: A volunteer special forces renowned as much for their expert reconnaissance capabilities as their bravery and combat prowess, the Rangers' primary purpose is dealing with threats the forces of the republic's standing Army cannot handle, as well as general peacekeeping around the borders of NCR territory, scouting out known and potential threats, and other special assignments. Their cutting-edge training, weaponry and assortment of both pre- and post-War technology often allows them to serve as lynchpins for victory in the republic's conflicts even when a situation may see them outnumbered and outgunned.
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_Rangers.label: NCR rangers
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_YearOfTheDragon.description: The Year of the Dragon ideology values national unity, collective effort, and technological progress. It encourages loyalty to the state, respect for tradition, and pride in cultural heritage. Citizens are expected to work together for the good of the nation, maintain social harmony, and embrace innovation, believing that unity and determination will bring prosperity and strength.
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_YearOfTheDragon.label: year of the dragon
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_TheAmericanDream.description: A shining promise of prosperity, freedom, and boundless opportunity, the American Dream fuels the hopes of millions across the nation. In these years, it is a vision of white-picket fences, two-car garages, and a future powered by atomic energy. Families gather around glowing televisions, confident that hard work and patriotism secure their place in a world of endless progress. Optimism runs high, and the Dream endures as a beacon of what America can achieve—an unwavering belief in a brighter tomorrow. Yet beneath the optimism, cracks form—corporate greed, resource shortages, and the shadow of war. Still, the Dream endures, a beacon of what America is meant to be, even as the world teeters on the edge of oblivion.
LanguageData.FCP_Meme_TheAmericanDream.label: the american dream
